# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: projectsService
  type: google::projects
  properties:
    projectId: ${PROJECT_ID}

- name: computeManager
  type: google::computeManager
  properties:
    project: ${PROJECT_ID}
    region: us-central1-a
    zone: us-central1-a
    serviceAccount: [DEFAULT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT]

- name: instanceTemplate
  type: google::computeInstanceTemplate
  properties:
    project: ${PROJECT_ID}
    region: us-central1
    zone: us-central1-a
    diskEncryptionKey: FIPS_KEY_NOT_SPECIFIED
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
      - deviceName: boot
        type: PERSISTENT
        boot: true
        autoDetect: true
        initializeParams:
          sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9

- name: instanceGroup
  type: google::computeInstanceGroup
  properties:
    project: ${PROJECT_ID}
    region: us-central1
    zone: us-central1-a
    instanceTemplate: $(instanceTemplate.name)
    namedPorts:
      - name: http
        port: 80
    instances: 3

- name: deployment
  type: google::computeDeploymentManagerHpa
  properties:
    project: ${PROJECT_ID}
    region: us-central1
    zone: us-central1-a
    deploymentName: my-deployment
    targetHttp2: 80
    instanceGroup: $(instanceGroup.name)
